title: atriplex rosea subsp. tarraconensis
intro: Atriplex rosea subsp. tarraconensis, also known as the Spanish saltwort, is a subspecies of shrubby plant that belongs to the Atriplex genus, which is commonly found in Mediterranean regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Atriplex rosea subsp. tarraconensis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce water retention, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and may also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal medicine.
